title: Tresendar Manor — Redbrand Hideout

location.name: Tresendar Manor — Cellar Complex
location.description: Beneath the ruined Tresendar Manor lies a multi-room cellar complex that the Redbrands have converted into their hideout. Cold stone corridors, storage rooms, and a natural cavern system make up the dungeon.
location.environment: dungeon, underground, dim light (torches in some rooms)

read_aloud: The cellar beneath Tresendar Manor is cold and damp. Rough-hewn stone walls line narrow corridors lit by flickering torches. The smell of stale beer and unwashed bodies hangs in the air. Somewhere deeper, you hear the low murmur of voices.

encounters.description: The Nothic lurks in a natural crevasse. It is not immediately hostile — it tries to communicate telepathically, bargaining for food (fresh meat) in exchange for information about Glasstaff.

enemies.position: In the natural crevasse, 20 feet below the bridge
enemies.special: Will trade information for food. Knows Glasstaff's real name is Iarno Albrek. Attacks only if threatened or refused.

encounters.tactics: The Nothic uses Weird Insight to learn party secrets and uses them as leverage. It wants fresh meat. If given food, it reveals that Glasstaff is really Iarno Albrek and that he has a secret escape route in his quarters. If attacked, it fights with Rotting Gaze.
encounters.description: The Nothic — a twisted aberration that was once a wizard. It can be negotiated with or fought.

enemies.position: Drinking and gambling at a table

encounters.tactics: Redbrands are caught off guard if the party enters quietly. They fight but surrender if outnumbered and wounded.
encounters.description: Three Redbrands in the common room.

encounters.tactics: If the party is stealthy (DC 10 Stealth), they can get a surprise round. Otherwise, the Redbrands grab weapons and fight.

enemies.special: Glasstaff — real name Iarno Albrek. Uses Staff of Defense (AC 18 with Shield, can cast Shield and Mage Armor). Has a secret escape route through a hidden door.

encounters.tactics: If Glasstaff hears combat, he tries to flee through the secret escape passage. If cornered, he casts Hold Person, then Magic Missile. He surrenders if reduced to 5 HP or less. He can reveal information about the Black Spider (Nezznar) and Wave Echo Cave.
encounters.description: Glasstaff (Iarno Albrek) — the Redbrand leader. A wizard working for the Black Spider.

npcs.name: Iarno Albrek (Glasstaff)
dialog_hooks: Real name is Iarno Albrek — the missing Lords' Alliance agent Sildar was looking for
Works for the Black Spider (Nezznar, a drow)
The Black Spider wants Wave Echo Cave and the Forge of Spells
Glasstaff was sent to Phandalin to keep the town under control and prevent interference
Has a letter from the Black Spider in his quarters — proof of the conspiracy

npcs.name: Mirna Dendrar
dialog_hooks: Imprisoned with her two children in the slave pens
Her husband Thel was murdered by the Redbrands for standing up to them
Offers the location of a family heirloom emerald necklace in the ruined herb shop in Thundertree as a reward

loot.location: Glasstaff's possession — +1 AC, can cast Shield (reaction) and Mage Armor
Glasstaff's desk — reveals the Black Spider's plans and Cragmaw Castle connection
Mirna reveals it's in the ruined herb shop in Thundertree

skill_checks.success: Notice the secret escape passage in Glasstaff's quarters before he can use it.
skill_checks.failure: Glasstaff may escape through the hidden door.

skill_checks.success: Approach the barracks or common room without alerting the Redbrands.
skill_checks.failure: Redbrands hear the party and prepare for a fight.

traps.name: Pit Trap
traps.trigger_description: A section of corridor floor is a concealed pit trap
traps.effect: Character falls 20 feet into a pit.
traps.disarm: Can be avoided by walking along the edges once detected

secrets.info: The Black Spider's letter to Glasstaff reads: 'Lord Albrek, my spies in Neverwinter tell me that strangers are due to arrive in Phandalin. They could be working for the dwarves. Capture them if you can, kill them if you must, but don't allow them to upset our plans. See that any dwarven maps in their possession are delivered to me with haste. I am counting on you, Iarno. Don't disappoint me. —The Black Spider'

dm_notes: This dungeon has multiple entry points — the secret tunnel (from Carp's info) or the manor cellar entrance. The Nothic encounter is a key decision point — fighting vs. negotiating teaches the party that not everything is a combat encounter. If Glasstaff escapes, he can reappear later at Cragmaw Castle. The letter from the Black Spider is the main plot hook connecting Chapters 2 and 3. Rescuing the Dendrars should feel heroic.
